items.title: in the finals, brunson is shooting:
items.author: Michael Pina
items.body_excerpt:
in the finals, brunson is shooting:
50% at the rim
21.1% from the mid-range
33.3% on non-corner 3's
—he has 13 assists and 13 turnovers, and his usage is up ~10% from first three rounds
—ny is -13 when on the court and +20 when off the court.

also: he's incredible! www.theringer.com/2026/06/09/n...

items.title: I find nothing more predictable and tedious in sports than the fans of the losing team blaming the officials after a playoff loss. It's lazy scapegoating and never explains why a team actually lost the game.
items.author: Kurt Helin
items.body_excerpt: I find nothing more predictable and tedious in sports than the fans of the losing team blaming the officials after a playoff loss. It's lazy scapegoating and never explains why a team actually lost the game.

items.title: During Mike Gansey’s hiring process, he kept asking a version of this question: Can we keep Jameer?
items.author: Gina Mizell
items.body_excerpt:
During Mike Gansey’s hiring process, he kept asking a version of this question: Can we keep Jameer? 

On Jameer Nelson’s promotion, and why doing it with his hometown Sixers matters.

“He has that equity with our players and the community”

www.inquirer.com/sixers/sixer...

media.title: Jameer Nelson wants to be ‘part of the solution’ for the Sixers. His promotion provides an opportunity to do that ‘at home.’
media.description: Nelson, a Chester native and former St. Joseph's star, now holds the No. 2 front office position under new president of basketball operations Mike Gansey, who was determined to keep him on staff.

items.title: Mentioned it in here, but I think Bridges picking up two quick fouls was a quiet ruiner for the Knicks. He's so important on both ends — his movement greasing the offense's skids, such a huge part of the defensive plan — that him basically getting reduced to zero was killer.
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: Mentioned it in here, but I think Bridges picking up two quick fouls was a quiet ruiner for the Knicks. He's so important on both ends — his movement greasing the offense's skids, such a huge part of the defensive plan — that him basically getting reduced to zero was killer.

items.title: we can dissect the game all we want, but ultimately, the vibes in new york were too sinister yesterday. the kalshification of viral "fans," trump caging off the garden. i saw the die hard knicks fans doing everything they could to restore the feeling but the dark cloud was too po
items.author: Molly Morrison
items.body_excerpt: we can dissect the game all we want, but ultimately, the vibes in new york were too sinister yesterday. the kalshification of viral "fans," trump caging off the garden. i saw the die hard knicks fans doing everything they could to restore the feeling but the dark cloud was too powerful.

items.title: The number of highly publicized and genuinely cruel stories that are going to surface during this World Cup are pretty much precisely the shame America deserves for its 250th birthday, if only the right people felt it.

items.title: June 9, 1985: Dick Raphael takes this photo of a Magic Johnson assist to James Worthy in Game 6 of the NBA Finals at Boston Garden.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 9, 1985: Dick Raphael takes this photo of a Magic Johnson assist to James Worthy in Game 6 of the NBA Finals at Boston Garden.

Magic had 14 PTS/14 AST, Worthy added 28 PTS and Finals MVP Kareem Abdul-Jabbar scored 29 PTS as the Lakers defeated the Celtics, 111-100, to win the NBA Championship.

items.title: Last night was the first time the Knicks committed more than 12 turnovers and dished out fewer than 20 assists since…
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Last night was the first time the Knicks committed more than 12 turnovers and dished out fewer than 20 assists since… 

yup, you guessed it, their last loss back on April 23rd to Atlanta in Game 3 of the first round.

items.title: It's funny, Sam Presti talked about this kind of thing Monday morning, but in terms of SGA criticism.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
It's funny, Sam Presti talked about this kind of thing Monday morning, but in terms of SGA criticism.

"The post-game press conference has turned into the bully pulpit to create competitive advantage...Everyone gets up there and they talk about the officials, and they discredit the other team."

media.title: Knicks squander golden opportunity, miss chance to take commanding NBA Finals lead
